Package simulation

Class NodeAgent

java.lang.Object
simulation.NodeAgent
public final class NodeAgent extends Object
A mutable sensor-node agent in the discrete-event simulation.

Replaces the inner Nodes.Node class from the legacy code. Unlike the algorithm layer's immutable SensorNode, a NodeAgent tracks the node's live state as it evolves through the simulation: current grid position, movement direction, speed, and the set of neighbour nodes it has directly encountered.

Per-instance caches — Bug E fix

The legacy Nodes.Node declared two static ArrayList fields (drone_cache_from_waypoint_with_node_caching and drone_direct_caching_with_node_caching), which were shared across ALL node instances. This completely broke per-node isolation. In this class every cache is a non-static instance field; each agent manages its own independent set.

Snapshot for the algorithm layer

Call toSensorNode() to obtain an immutable SensorNode snapshot reflecting the agent's current position. This snapshot is safe to pass to the algorithm layer and is never affected by subsequent position updates.

  • Field Details

    • DIRECTION_COUNT

      public static final int DIRECTION_COUNT
      Number of supported movement directions (N/S/E/W/NE/NW/SE/SW).
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

  • Constructor Details

    • NodeAgent

      public NodeAgent(int id, int x, int y, int direction, int speed)
      Constructs a sensor-node agent with the given identity and initial state.
      Parameters:
      id - unique node identifier (assigned from nodeIdStart)
      x - initial row index on the grid
      y - initial column index on the grid
      direction - initial movement direction (0–7)
      speed - movement speed in grid cells per tick (determines inter-event interval: next event scheduled at currentTime + speed)

  • Method Details

    • getId

      public int getId()
      Returns:
      unique node ID

    • getX

      public int getX()
      Returns:
      current row index on the grid

    • getY

      public int getY()
      Returns:
      current column index on the grid

    • getDirection

      public int getDirection()
      Returns:
      current movement direction (0–7)

    • getSpeed

      public int getSpeed()
      Returns:
      movement speed (cells per tick, also used as inter-event gap)

    • updatePosition

      public void updatePosition(int newX, int newY)
      Updates the agent's grid position. Should only be called by NodeMover after a successful grid-cell move.
      Parameters:
      newX - new row index
      newY - new column index

    • setDirection

      public void setDirection(int direction)
      Sets the agent's movement direction.
      Parameters:
      direction - new direction (0–7)

    • addNeighbor

      public void addNeighbor(int nodeId)
      Records that this agent encountered another node with the given ID. Duplicate entries are silently ignored.
      Parameters:
      nodeId - ID of the encountered neighbour

    • hasNeighbor

      public boolean hasNeighbor(int nodeId)
      Returns true if this agent has previously encountered the node with the given ID.
      Parameters:
      nodeId - ID to check
      Returns:
      true if nodeId is in this agent's cache

    • getNeighborCache

      public Set<Integer> getNeighborCache()
      Returns an unmodifiable view of this agent's neighbour cache.

      The iteration order reflects the order in which neighbours were first encountered (insertion order of the underlying LinkedHashSet).

      Returns:
      unmodifiable set of neighbour node IDs

    • toSensorNode

      public SensorNode toSensorNode()
      Creates an immutable SensorNode snapshot from this agent's current position, direction, and speed.

      The returned snapshot is independent of this agent; subsequent position or direction changes do not affect it. The velocity information (direction + speed) allows the UAV to compute a predicted interception point rather than chasing the node's last-known static location.

      Pass the snapshot to the algorithm layer (PathPlanner) via the KnowledgeBase.

      Returns:
      immutable snapshot of this agent's current state including velocity

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Overrides:
      toString in class Object
